purpose: Test whether aggressive trade flow contains repeatable net forward information when observed in completed five-second buckets rather than one-minute averages. The daily-return target is excluded from rule selection.

sampling.rule: mechanical first three UTC calendar days of every quarter

raw_data.source: Binance USD-M public daily aggTrades archive

research_clock.entry: first observable trade price of the next completed bucket with at least one full bucket latency
research_clock.cross_day_state: reset at each sampled UTC day

hypotheses.continuation: large normalized aggressive-flow imbalance with efficient same-direction price impact continues over 30 seconds to 30 minutes
hypotheses.absorption: large aggressive-flow imbalance with low or opposite price impact reverses after the first completed confirmation bucket
hypotheses.quarter_hour: the first completed five or ten seconds after each UTC quarter-hour contains incremental information not visible in one-minute bars
hypotheses.impact_decay: flow-normalized price impact decays at a state-dependent rate and only the residual component is tradable
